metadata.basedOn: Original 31-listing analysis cross-referenced against updated resume, portfolio (karolbuczek.com), and case studies
metadata.repositioningStatus: In progress — significant improvements since v1.0
metadata.overallReadiness: 78% — ready to apply to pre-sales and integration SA roles now; cloud-native and enterprise SA roles require cert completion and Azure depth

strengths.label: Rare dual background — marketing leadership + engineering depth
strengths.detail: Former Marketing Director who scaled a company from 1 to 4 markets AND built the systems that ran it. This combination is explicitly sought in SA job descriptions (25+ of 31 listings) and almost never found in candidates. John's testimonial directly validates this in language that mirrors SA job description language.

strengths.label: Pre-sales capability is production-proven, not theoretical
strengths.detail: 15+ years of freelance and consulting work means real discovery calls, real scope documents, real POC delivery, and real deal closure. Not adjacent experience — actual pre-sales activity. This is the highest-compensated SA sub-type ($130K–$200K) and the strongest fit.

strengths.label: Integration breadth is genuinely enterprise-level
strengths.detail: Five9, ServiceTitan, Lead Perfection, Mailchimp, Marketo, SendGrid, Microsoft Dynamics, Twilio, Hatch, BirdEye, Get the Referral, WooCommerce, GTM, GA4 — this is a real enterprise integration portfolio, not a demo reel. Most SA candidates can't name 15 platforms they've actually integrated.

strengths.label: Portfolio is live, case-study backed, and architecturally credible
strengths.detail: karolbuczek.com now has a coherent SA identity — hero, three-pillar structure, four case studies with problem/solution/impact framing, and a live interactive resume. Most candidates have a PDF. You have a credibility system.

strengths.label: AI angle is production-backed, not badge-dropping
strengths.detail: Locally hosted HuggingFace Whisper pipeline is a real production system that solves a real compliance problem. This differentiates from the 90% of candidates who list 'AI' and mean 'I use ChatGPT.'

strengths.label: Multi-cloud exposure now documented
strengths.detail: AWS (production), GCP (production Python ETL into Databricks), Azure (AZ-900 cert). Three cloud platforms on record — upgraded from AWS-only in v1.0.

strengths.label: Positioning is now consistent across all touchpoints
strengths.detail: LinkedIn title, portfolio headline, resume title, and case study framing all say the same thing: Principal Solutions Architect. No mixed signals between 'web developer' and 'architect' anymore.

weaknesses.label: AWS SAA-C03 still in progress
weaknesses.detail: Appears as a requirement or preference in 10 of 31 listings. The cert being in-progress is noted correctly everywhere, but until it's complete it remains a filter risk in ATS systems that screen for certification keywords.

weaknesses.label: No production Azure experience
weaknesses.detail: AZ-900 is a fundamentals cert — it signals awareness, not capability. Azure appears in 13 of 31 listings. For Microsoft-ecosystem roles (Perficient, Cyclotron, consulting firms) this is a hard blocker.

weaknesses.label: No Kubernetes or Terraform
weaknesses.detail: Both appear in 8 of 31 listings. Not universal but increasingly expected at the $150K+ level for cloud-native SA roles. Currently a gap with no partial credit.

weaknesses.label: Python is functional, not advanced
weaknesses.detail: Python appears in 14 of 31 listings. The ETL pipeline on GCP closes the gap partially, but advanced Python (FastAPI, async patterns, ML tooling) is expected in AI-forward and data-heavy SA roles.

weaknesses.label: No formal Salesforce or HubSpot enterprise CRM experience
weaknesses.detail: Appears in 5 of 31 listings. Not universal but a blocker for CRM-ecosystem SA roles. ServiceTitan and Lead Perfection are strong industry-specific CRM depth but don't substitute for Salesforce in most enterprise contexts.

weaknesses.label: Case study visuals are placeholder
weaknesses.detail: The portfolio cards still show grey placeholder images. For a design-conscious portfolio this is the most immediately visible gap — it signals incomplete work to anyone visiting the case studies page.

weaknesses.label: No Braze, Segment, or modern CDP experience
weaknesses.detail: Increasingly referenced in MarTech SA roles. Custom CDP work (MHAT platform) is adjacent but not equivalent to hands-on experience with enterprise CDPs.

opportunities.label: Pre-sales SA roles are the highest-fit, highest-pay segment
opportunities.detail: Cyara ($180K–$200K), IDEMIA ($130K–$170K), CrowdStrike ($135K–$205K), Stitch Integration Architect ($120K–$150K estimated) — these roles weight business communication, demo delivery, and requirements translation far more than IaC or Kubernetes. Strongest immediate opportunity.

opportunities.label: MarTech SA is an underserved niche with premium pay
opportunities.detail: Few candidates combine deep MarTech integration experience (GTM, attribution, CDP, lifecycle automation) with cloud architecture skills. The Stitch cover letter is a strong proof point for this positioning.

opportunities.label: AWS SAA-C03 completion is a near-term multiplier
opportunities.detail: Completing this cert closes the single most common screening gap. It upgrades match from partial to true on 10 listings immediately.

opportunities.label: ServiceTitan depth is a vertical SA differentiator
opportunities.detail: Home services is a massive, underserved vertical for SA talent. Companies like ServiceTitan, Threshold Brands, and field service SaaS platforms actively need SAs with real ServiceTitan integration depth. Worth a targeted search.

opportunities.label: AI case study closes a growing demand gap
opportunities.detail: AI Engineering is showing up in more SA listings every month. The local inference pipeline case study — once properly documented with visuals — becomes a genuine differentiator in a market where most SAs have zero production AI work.

opportunities.label: Integration SA at SaaS companies is wide open
opportunities.detail: Smarsh, Remote.com, and similar SaaS-native companies are actively hiring Integration Architects. The UWD case study maps directly to this sub-type.

threats.label: AWS cert in-progress may not survive ATS filtering
threats.detail: Many ATS systems filter for 'AWS Certified' as a keyword. In-progress status doesn't trigger this filter. Every week without the cert is a week of potential resume drops.

threats.label: Azure gap blocks a large segment of the market
threats.detail: 13 of 31 listings require Azure. That's 42% of the market currently inaccessible without at minimum an AZ-104 (Azure Administrator) to demonstrate hands-on capability beyond AZ-900.

threats.label: Consulting/founder framing can raise 'employability' concerns
threats.detail: Some hiring managers see 'Self-employed' and wonder whether the candidate can operate inside a structure, follow direction, and collaborate on a team. The portfolio and resume now mitigate this — but it remains a potential screening bias.

threats.label: Salary floor ($120K+) filters out a large share of open listings
threats.detail: Many remote SA listings in the $100K–$120K range would be strong fits but fall below stated minimum. Being rigid on floor may extend timeline unnecessarily.

threats.label: Placeholder portfolio visuals could undermine credibility
threats.detail: A hiring manager who clicks through from the resume to the portfolio and sees grey placeholder images may form a negative impression that overrides strong copy. High-risk, easy fix.

currentStateAnalysis.repositioningProgress: Strong. The v1.0 assessment identified 65% alignment. Current state is approximately 78% — driven primarily by multi-cloud documentation (GCP production work added), portfolio coherence (all touchpoints now aligned), AI case study addition, and certification progress (AZ-900, GCP-DL completed).
readyToApplyNow: Integration Architect roles at SaaS and MarTech companies ($120K–$160K)
MarTech Solution Architect roles at agencies and consulting firms ($120K–$150K)
Implementation Architect roles at professional services firms ($120K–$150K)
notReadyYet: Microsoft-ecosystem SA roles requiring production Azure ($130K–$180K)
Enterprise SA roles at F500 companies requiring TOGAF or PMP
AI/ML SA roles requiring SageMaker, Vertex AI, or MLOps experience
topThreeActionsToIncreaseReadiness: Complete AWS SAA-C03 — highest ROI single action, closes 10 listing gaps immediately
Replace portfolio placeholder images with real screenshots — highest visibility fix, zero technical effort
Build one Azure project (Logic Apps + Azure Functions) to go from AZ-900 to partial Azure match — opens 13 additional listings
improvementsSinceV1: GCP match upgraded from false to partial — production Python ETL pipeline into Databricks now documented
Azure match upgraded from false to partial — AZ-900 and GCP-DL certifications completed
AI Engineering match upgraded from partial to true — HuggingFace Whisper production pipeline documented as case study
Multi-cloud match upgraded from false to partial — AWS + GCP production + Azure fundamentals
Portfolio positioning upgraded from mixed to coherent — all touchpoints now consistent SA identity
Pre-sales match strengthened — Stitch cover letter demonstrates ability to articulate pre-sales narrative confidently
MarTech platform depth expanded — ServiceTitan, Lead Perfection, Marketo, Twilio, Hatch, BirdEye, Get the Referral added to record

preSales.label: Architect client-specific solutions with sales teams
preSales.description: Work closely with sales teams to architect and propose solutions that address prospective client requirements, demonstrating platform value and differentiators
preSales.portfolioEvidence: Tall Karol description, Stitch cover letter, 15+ years consulting

preSales.label: Develop and deliver customized demos and POCs
preSales.description: Develop and present customized demonstrations and proof-of-concept solutions to prospective clients, showcasing technical capabilities aligned to their use cases
preSales.portfolioEvidence: Explicitly documented in resume soft skills and Tall Karol role description

preSales.label: Lead technical discovery and requirements gathering
preSales.description: Lead technical discovery sessions with client stakeholders to gather requirements, understand business goals, and translate them into actionable solution designs
preSales.portfolioEvidence: Documented in resume, homepage, and Stitch cover letter

preSales.label: Respond to RFPs/RFIs with technical proposals
preSales.description: Oversee RFP/RFI responses, craft detailed technical solution proposals, and provide accurate project scoping and estimation for statements of work
preSales.note: SOW experience documented but formal RFP process not explicitly called out — consider adding to resume

preSales.label: Qualify opportunities and define solution approaches
preSales.description: Collaborate with sales leadership to qualify opportunities, define technical approaches, and identify architectural risks during the presales cycle

preSales.label: Support contract finalization and delivery handoff
preSales.description: Facilitate smooth transition from sales to implementation, ensuring clear communication of proposed solutions, scope boundaries, and client expectations to delivery teams

preSales.label: Achieve or support revenue and consumption targets
preSales.description: Contribute to achieving quarterly revenue targets by identifying new opportunities for expansion, upselling, and driving platform adoption within accounts

integration.label: Design end-to-end integration architectures
integration.description: Design end-to-end integration architectures connecting multiple platforms, systems, and data sources using APIs, middleware, and iPaaS tools
integration.portfolioEvidence: UWD case study, MHAT case study, resume integration layer description

integration.label: Integrate with enterprise systems and third-party platforms
integration.description: Oversee integration with enterprise applications (ERP, CRM, OMS), payment gateways, third-party vendors, and external APIs, ensuring data integrity and performance
integration.portfolioEvidence: ServiceTitan, Lead Perfection, Five9, Microsoft Dynamics, Mailchimp, Marketo, Twilio, BirdEye, Get the Referral — documented

integration.label: Define integration standards and governance models
integration.description: Establish integration standards, reference architectures, API governance models, and reusable patterns to ensure consistency, scalability, and maintainability
integration.note: Shared logging framework at UWD is direct evidence — consider calling this out more explicitly

integration.label: Architect API management and gateway solutions
integration.description: Architect API management solutions including gateway configuration, security policies, versioning, rate limiting, documentation standards, and analytics integration

integration.label: Implement data mappings and transformation logic
integration.description: Implement complex data mappings, transformations (JSON, XML, XSLT, CSV), and ETL/ELT pipelines to ensure data integrity across integrated systems
integration.note: Upgraded from partial to true — GCP Python ETL pipeline into Databricks is direct evidence

integration.label: Lead platform migration strategies
integration.description: Lead discovery, assessment, and migration planning from on-premises middleware to cloud integration platforms, including cutover strategies and rollback plans
integration.note: Joomla to WordPress migration with zero downtime is partial evidence — not middleware migration but demonstrates migration planning

stakeholderManagement.label: Translate business requirements into technical designs
stakeholderManagement.description: Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather technical and functional requirements, understand business goals, and translate them into detailed architectural designs

stakeholderManagement.label: Present to executive and technical audiences
stakeholderManagement.description: Present architecture designs, technical trade-offs, and solution options to diverse audiences including senior executives, engineering teams, and external partners

stakeholderManagement.label: Serve as trusted technical advisor
stakeholderManagement.description: Act as a trusted technology advisor to clients and internal leadership, framing decisions in business terms and clearly explaining options, tradeoffs, and risk
stakeholderManagement.portfolioEvidence: John Kosmides testimonial is direct third-party validation of this capability

stakeholderManagement.label: Bridge business and engineering across teams
stakeholderManagement.description: Act as key technical leader bridging business needs and engineering execution, coordinating across product, sales, engineering, and operations teams

stakeholderManagement.label: Manage vendor and partner technical relationships
stakeholderManagement.description: Serve as technical point of contact for third-party vendors, integration partners, and technology alliances, resolving issues and driving collaborative outcomes

architectureDesign.label: Design scalable, secure cloud-native architectures
architectureDesign.description: Design scalable, reliable, secure, and cost-effective cloud infrastructure and application architectures for enterprise and customer-facing environments
architectureDesign.note: AWS production work is strong. Multi-cloud now has GCP + Azure fundamentals. Kubernetes/IaC still missing for full match.

architectureDesign.label: Define technology roadmaps aligned to business strategy
architectureDesign.description: Develop architecture and technology roadmaps aligned with business objectives, including phased transformation plans and multi-year strategy

architectureDesign.label: Evaluate and recommend emerging technologies
architectureDesign.description: Evaluate emerging cloud technologies, industry trends, and platform updates, recommending adoption strategies and assessing impact on existing architectures
architectureDesign.portfolioEvidence: AI tooling adoption, local inference pipeline, GCP/Databricks selection documented

architectureDesign.label: Create reference architectures and solution playbooks
architectureDesign.description: Create and scale reference architectures, solution playbooks, reusable patterns, and architectural guardrails for repeatable engagements

architectureDesign.label: Architect for high availability and disaster recovery
architectureDesign.description: Define architecture for high availability, data redundancy, data loss prevention, site recovery, resilience, and business continuity at the architecture level

documentation.label: Create architecture diagrams and design documents
documentation.description: Create and maintain detailed architecture diagrams, network diagrams, design documents, and operational procedures using standard notation and frameworks
documentation.note: Case studies now include placeholder for architecture diagrams — filling these in with real diagrams would strengthen this claim significantly

documentation.label: Document solution specs, data flows, and API requirements
documentation.description: Define and maintain deployment specifications, data flow diagrams, API requirements, integration documentation, and solution acceptance criteria

documentation.label: Develop reusable playbooks and enablement content
documentation.description: Develop reusable solution playbooks, demo frameworks, best practice guides, case studies, and technical enablement content for internal and partner use
documentation.note: The portfolio itself is partial evidence of this — case studies are repeatable frameworks. Worth calling out.

cloud.label: AWS
cloud.description: Amazon Web Services — Lambda, S3, RDS, Cognito, Amplify in production. Most frequently required cloud platform.

cloud.label: Microsoft Azure
cloud.description: Azure cloud platform. AZ-900 completed. No production Azure work yet.
cloud.changeFromV1: upgraded — was false, now partial due to AZ-900 completion
cloud.note: AZ-104 or a small Azure project would upgrade to stronger partial. Full match requires production deployment.

cloud.label: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
cloud.description: GCP — production Python ETL middleware pipeline deployed. GCP Digital Leader cert completed.
cloud.changeFromV1: upgraded — was false, now partial due to production ETL work and GCP-DL cert

cloud.label: Multi-cloud architecture
cloud.description: AWS (production) + GCP (production) + Azure (fundamentals) — three cloud platforms now on record

cloud.label: Serverless (Lambda, Functions)
cloud.description: AWS Lambda in production for document portal and automation workflows

apisAndIntegration.label: REST API design and development
apisAndIntegration.description: Designing, building, and consuming RESTful APIs. Most universally required integration skill.

apisAndIntegration.label: Enterprise platform integration breadth
apisAndIntegration.description: ServiceTitan, Lead Perfection, Microsoft Dynamics, Five9, Mailchimp, Marketo, SendGrid, Twilio, Hatch, BirdEye, Get the Referral, WooCommerce, Zapier — 13+ enterprise platforms integrated at API level
apisAndIntegration.changeFromV1: upgraded — platform count and depth significantly expanded
apisAndIntegration.note: This is a genuine differentiator. Very few SA candidates can name 13 platforms they've actually integrated.

apisAndIntegration.label: API management platforms
apisAndIntegration.description: Custom API gateway patterns built — no formal Apigee/Kong/MuleSoft experience

apisAndIntegration.label: Integration patterns (pub/sub, event-driven, orchestration)
apisAndIntegration.description: Webhook-based and event-driven patterns implemented. Formal pub/sub (Kafka, EventBridge) not documented.

apisAndIntegration.label: Microservices architecture
apisAndIntegration.description: Modular architecture patterns applied — SubZap modularization, shared logging framework — but not formal microservices decomposition

apisAndIntegration.label: iPaaS platforms (Boomi, MuleSoft, Workato)
apisAndIntegration.description: Advanced Zapier (modular SubZap architecture) is the closest analog — enterprise iPaaS tools not in stack
apisAndIntegration.note: Reframe Zapier expertise as iPaaS architecture in interviews

data.label: SQL and relational databases
data.description: Advanced MySQL — data warehouse design, complex querying, stored procedures across multiple systems

data.label: Data warehousing and analytics platforms
data.description: Custom MySQL data warehouse (PPW) + Databricks via Python ETL pipeline (GCP). Two real warehouse implementations.

data.label: Data migration and ETL/ELT
data.description: Python ETL middleware on GCP into Databricks — production implementation

data.label: Data modeling and architecture
data.description: MySQL schema design, data warehouse architecture, event schema design for GTM

data.label: BI and dashboard tools
data.description: Custom React-based dashboards built — no Power BI or Tableau experience

data.label: AI/ML data pipelines
data.description: HuggingFace Whisper local inference pipeline is the closest ML work. No SageMaker, Vertex AI, or MLOps.
data.changeFromV1: upgraded — was false, now partial due to production AI pipeline

languages.label: Python
languages.description: Functional to intermediate — GCP ETL pipeline, HuggingFace tooling, local AI workflows. Not advanced.

languages.label: JavaScript / TypeScript

languages.label: SQL (advanced)
languages.description: Advanced MySQL — data warehouse design, complex queries, stored procedures

languages.label: PHP
languages.description: Advanced — custom REST APIs, automation frameworks, CRM integrations. Not appearing in SA listings.
languages.note: Reframe as 'server-side API development and integration engineering' in SA contexts

softSkills.label: Executive communication and presentation
softSkills.description: Presented to C-suite at orgs up to 3,000+ employees. Third-party validated by John Kosmides testimonial.

softSkills.label: Cross-functional collaboration
softSkills.description: Marketing Director leading teams of 5 across marketing, sales, operations. 15+ years client-facing consulting.

softSkills.label: Business acumen and strategic thinking
softSkills.description: Former Marketing Director — scaled company 1 to 4 markets. Understands P&L, campaigns, operational friction.

softSkills.label: Consultative selling approach
softSkills.description: 15+ years freelancing and consulting — discovery, scoping, POC, deal closure

softSkills.label: Influence without authority
softSkills.description: Consulting model requires leading without line authority — consistently documented

toolsAndPlatforms.label: CI/CD pipelines
toolsAndPlatforms.description: No formal CI/CD pipeline experience documented
toolsAndPlatforms.note: Quick win — set up GitHub Actions on an existing project and document it

toolsAndPlatforms.label: Git version control

toolsAndPlatforms.label: Monitoring and observability
toolsAndPlatforms.description: Custom logging framework at UWD is partial evidence. No Datadog, Splunk, or New Relic.

martech.note: Rare differentiator in SA market. Position as domain expertise in MarTech and revenue technology.

martech.label: Customer engagement platforms (Braze, Segment, Iterable)
martech.description: No experience with enterprise CDP or customer engagement platforms
martech.note: MHAT platform (custom CDP) is adjacent — acknowledge gap, position custom CDP work as architecture-level understanding

ai.description: Production HuggingFace Whisper pipeline — locally hosted, privacy-compliant, multi-language, automated output generation
ai.changeFromV1: upgraded — was partial, now true with production case study
ai.portfolioEvidence: Full case study documented at karolbuczek.com/case-studies

ai.description: Active use of LLM tooling for analysis, documentation, and system iteration

ai.description: No experience with enterprise ML frameworks

qualificationsAndCerts.description: For principal, lead, or staff-level SA positions

qualificationsAndCerts.label: Client-facing or consulting experience
qualificationsAndCerts.description: Proven experience in client-facing roles, technical consulting, or professional services

qualificationsAndCerts.note: In progress — listed correctly on all touchpoints. Complete ASAP.

qualificationsAndCerts.description: Two or more full end-to-end implementation lifecycles
qualificationsAndCerts.portfolioEvidence: MHAT, Bliss, UWD integration — all documented as full lifecycle

resumeBullets.label: Enterprise integration breadth
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Architected and maintained API integration layer connecting [X]+ enterprise platforms — including Five9, ServiceTitan, Lead Perfection, Microsoft Dynamics, Mailchimp, Marketo, and Twilio — creating a single source of truth across sales, marketing, and operations

resumeBullets.label: Pre-sales scoping and POC delivery
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Led [X]+ client engagements from technical discovery through POC delivery and production deployment — conducting stakeholder interviews, defining architecture scope, and authoring statements of work with [X]% on-scope delivery rate

resumeBullets.label: AWS cloud architecture — document portal
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Architected and deployed a RBAC-secured document portal on AWS (Amplify, Cognito, Lambda, S3, RDS) supporting [X] concurrent users — replacing a manual email-based process and providing audit-ready visibility into every file interaction

resumeBullets.label: Python ETL on GCP into Databricks
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Built a Python ETL middleware layer on GCP connecting raw operational data sources to a Databricks data warehouse — enabling analytics-ready tables for reporting and forecasting workflows

resumeBullets.label: Local AI inference pipeline
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Designed and deployed a locally-hosted AI transcription system using HuggingFace Whisper — processing sensitive meeting audio entirely on-premise with zero external data transmission, multi-language support, and automated deadline extraction with ICS calendar output

resumeBullets.label: Business scaling through technology
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Scaled marketing technology and operations during company expansion from 1 to 4 markets as Marketing Director — owning the full technology stack, building internal tooling, and achieving 237% conversion rate increase and 175% CTR improvement through systematic A/B testing

resumeBullets.label: Dual business and engineering background
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Uniquely positioned at the intersection of full-stack engineering and marketing leadership — understand what systems need to do for the business, not just how to build them. [Validated by VP-level reference: 'bridges the gap between deep technical capability and real business understanding']

resumeBullets.label: MarTech attribution architecture
resumeBullets.bulletTemplate: Built a UTM persistence and multi-touch attribution engine connecting web, CRM, and marketing platforms — giving marketing teams reliable first-touch and last-touch data for the first time and enabling accurate campaign ROI measurement

gapAnalysis.label: AWS SAA-C03 certification
gapAnalysis.description: In progress. Required or preferred in 10 of 31 listings. Highest single ROI action remaining.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Complete immediately. Until done, include 'AWS Solutions Architect Associate (SAA-C03) — expected [month] 2026' on all materials.

gapAnalysis.label: Production Azure experience
gapAnalysis.description: AZ-900 completed but no production deployment. Azure appears in 13 of 31 listings.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Build one small Azure project — deploy a Function App + Logic App integration. Document it. That's enough to move from 'fundamentals only' to 'hands-on' in interviews.
gapAnalysis.changeFromV1: downgraded from high-gap to partial — AZ-900 completion is meaningful progress but doesn't close the gap

gapAnalysis.label: Portfolio placeholder images
gapAnalysis.description: Grey placeholder images on case study cards are the most visible credibility gap for anyone clicking through from a resume or application.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Take screenshots of actual dashboards, architecture diagrams, or UI from each project. Even a hand-drawn architecture diagram photographed and cleaned up is better than a grey box. This is a same-day fix.

gapAnalysis.label: Kubernetes and container orchestration
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 8 of 31 listings. No experience documented.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Containerize an existing project with Docker, deploy to ECS or EKS. Document in portfolio. KodeKloud has a free Kubernetes basics course. Not urgent but closes a consistent gap.

gapAnalysis.label: Terraform / Infrastructure as Code
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 8 of 31 listings. No IaC experience.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Write Terraform configs for existing AWS infrastructure. Add to portfolio. The HashiCorp Terraform Associate cert is straightforward and well-recognized.

gapAnalysis.label: CI/CD pipeline experience
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 14 of 31 listings. Not currently documented.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Set up GitHub Actions on an existing portfolio project. Document it. This is a 2-4 hour task that closes a gap appearing in nearly half of all listings.

gapAnalysis.label: Python — advanced proficiency
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 14 of 31 listings. Currently functional to intermediate.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Build a FastAPI-based REST service or extend the ETL pipeline with additional transformation logic. Functional Python is sufficient for most SA roles — advanced only needed for AI/ML-heavy positions.

gapAnalysis.label: Enterprise CRM (Salesforce / HubSpot)
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 5 of 31 listings. ServiceTitan and Lead Perfection are strong vertical substitutes but not equivalent.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Complete Salesforce Trailhead basics (free). Add to LinkedIn. ServiceTitan depth is more valuable for field service verticals — lead with that for industry-specific roles.

gapAnalysis.label: Modern CDP / customer engagement platforms (Braze, Segment)
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 4 of 31 listings, concentrated in MarTech SA roles.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Explore Segment's free tier. The MHAT platform (custom CDP architecture) demonstrates architectural understanding — position that in interviews.

gapAnalysis.label: Formal Agile / Scrum certification
gapAnalysis.recommendation: PSM I is the fastest and cheapest path if needed. Low priority relative to cloud certs.

gapAnalysis.label: Java proficiency
gapAnalysis.description: Appears in 6 of 31 listings, primarily enterprise and cybersecurity SA roles.
gapAnalysis.recommendation: Not worth pursuing unless targeting Java-ecosystem enterprise roles specifically. JS/TS/PHP demonstrates polyglot capability.
